diagrams.title: Parity Bit Calculation
diagrams.description: Shows how a single parity bit is added to a 7-bit data word to enable error detection. Demonstrates the concept of even and odd parity checking.
elements: 7 data bits arranged in a row
diagrams.purpose: Explains the fundamental concept of parity checking, the simplest form of error detection, showing how redundant bits are added to data for validation

diagrams.title: Hamming Code Structure
diagrams.description: Illustrates the positioning of redundant bits (parity bits) within a data word and how they protect different groups of data bits. Shows the relationship between data bits and parity bit coverage.
elements: Positions labeled P1 P2 D1 P3 D2 D3 D4 P4 D5 D6 D7 D8
Groupings shown with different colored lines connecting parity bits to their protected data bits
Table showing which data bits each parity bit covers
diagrams.purpose: Explains the Hamming code error correction mechanism, showing how multiple parity bits are positioned and how they create overlapping protection groups to locate and correct single-bit errors

diagrams.title: CRC Polynomial Division
diagrams.description: Demonstrates the Cyclic Redundancy Check process showing the binary division of the data frame by a generator polynomial, resulting in a remainder that becomes the CRC code.
elements: Division process visualization with XOR operations
diagrams.purpose: Explains the CRC error detection method, showing how polynomial division works to generate a checksum that can detect multiple bit errors and is widely used in data link layers
